description: DALP appendix diagram deck — 6 architecture and process diagrams covering token issuance, compliance, platform layers, asset lifecycle, maker-checker, and integration topology

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Architecture and process diagrams for technical reference

slides.title: Token issuance flow
slides.body: End-to-end token issuance on DALP: investor portal initiates a subscription, DALP runs identity and whitelist checks, orchestrates the mint on the smart contract, and credits the investor's custody wallet — all in a single governed flow.

slides.title: Compliance check sequence
slides.body:
Every action on DALP — transfer, issuance, or redemption — passes through a layered compliance gate before touching on-chain state.

• Identity check: DID and KYC status confirmed
• Whitelist check: counterparty must be on the approved register
• Policy engine: transaction rules and jurisdictional constraints applied
• Limit check: holding caps and velocity limits enforced

Blocked actions are rejected with a logged reason before any transaction is submitted, keeping the audit trail clean and preventing on-chain state from diverging from compliance intent.

slides.title: DALP platform architecture layers
slides.body:
DALP is structured in four distinct layers, each with a clear responsibility boundary:

• Channel layer: investor portals, REST APIs, and the operator console — all entry points into the platform
• Business services: asset lifecycle management, compliance modules, identity and KYC orchestration, and post-issuance servicing
• Execution layer: orchestration engine, maker-checker workflow, event bus, and policy enforcement
• Blockchain layer: EVM-compatible smart contracts, token standards (ERC-20/3643), and on-chain state

This layered model allows operators to integrate at any level without rebuilding the compliance and execution logic that sits below it.

slides.title: Digital asset lifecycle
slides.body: From initial configuration through issuance, distribution, ongoing servicing, atomic DvP settlement, and final redemption — DALP manages the complete lifecycle of a digital asset, not just the token mint.

slides.title: Maker-checker approval flow
slides.body:
DALP enforces institutional-grade separation of duties through a configurable maker-checker pattern — required by regulators for tokenized asset operations.

• Maker initiates the action and submits a draft record
• Draft enters a review queue with full context and audit metadata
• Checker independently reviews — a different user, different role
• Checker approves or rejects with a mandatory reason
• Approved actions execute on-chain atomically
• Rejected actions return to the maker with documented reasoning

The entire sequence is immutably logged. No on-chain action can bypass this gate when maker-checker is configured on an asset class or operation type. This is not optional theatre — it is a hard control in the execution layer.

slides.title: Integration topology
slides.body: DALP acts as an integration hub between enterprise back-office systems — CRM, OMS, ERP, treasury, and external KYC providers — and the execution layer of smart contracts and custodians. Outputs connect to settlement rails, CSD networks, and T+0 DvP infrastructure.
